Program_Name: Alliance for Innovation on Maternal Health
Program_Description: A program focused on improving maternal health safety and quality in birthing hospitals.
Target_Population: Birthing hospitals and obstetric providers; indirectly serves all pregnant women

Program_Description: A program that provides home visits to expectant parents and families with young children.
Target_Population: Expectant parents and families with children from birth through age 3 (or 5 in some cases)

Program_Name: Special Supplemental Nutrition Program for Women, Infants, and Children
Program_Description: A nutrition program that provides assistance to low-income pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women, infants, and children.
Target_Population: Low-income pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women; infants and children

Program_Description: Addresses the health disparities faced by Black/African American women and their infants.
Target_Population: Black/African American women of childbearing age

Program_Description: Conducts retrospective reviews of fetal and infant deaths to improve health outcomes.
Provides home visiting services to pregnant women and parents with young children.
Target_Population: Pregnant women and parents with children 0-5 in at-risk communities

Program_Description: This program provides social support, education classes, case management, referral and assistance into care for eligible high-risk families and infants up to one year of age.
Target_Population: Black/African American women of childbearing age and their infants

Program_Description: Healthy Families focuses on building and nurturing parent-child relationships and interactions through in-home and virtual visits.
Target_Population: Pregnant individuals or those with an infant less than 3 months old
Eligibility_Requirements: Eligibility criteria: pregnant or those who have an infant less than 3 months old.

Program_Description: WIC helps families by providing nutrition education, breastfeeding support, healthy foods and referrals to health care and other community services.
Target_Population: Pregnant and post-partum women, newborn infants, and children under the age of 5
Application_Process: Contact main WIC office for information

Program_Description: Home visits for high risk families and infants for care referrals and coordination, developmental assessments and parent education.
Addresses the higher rates of infant mortality among Black/African American women.
Target_Population: Black/African American women of childbearing age

Program_Description: Conducts retrospective reviews of fetal and infant deaths to improve health outcomes.

Program_Name: Maternal, Infant, and Early Childhood Home Visiting Program
Program_Description: Provides home visiting services to pregnant women and parents with young children.
Target_Population: Pregnant women and parents with children 0-5 in at-risk communities

Program_Description: Supports high-risk pregnant women on Medi-Cal with specialized care.
